source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in the cyclic pentagon $ABCDE$, if $\angle C + \angle CDE + \angle E + \angle EAB = 425^\circ$, what is the measure of $\angle CDA$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since the sum of the interior angles of pentagon $ABCDE$ is $(5-2)\times 180^\circ=540^\circ$, \\
thus $\angle EAB+\angle B+\angle C+\angle CDE+\angle E=540^\circ$, \\
because $\angle EAB+\angle C+\angle CDE+\angle E=425^\circ$, \\
therefore $\angle B=540^\circ-425^\circ=115^\circ$, \\
since quadrilateral $ABCD$ is inscribed in circle $O$, \\
therefore $\angle B+\angle CDA=180^\circ$, \\
thus $\angle CDA=180^\circ-115^\circ=\boxed{65^\circ}$.

the target angle is not read directly; it is derived by combining given angle information with geometric constraints
verifiability: the final answer is a single numeric angle value (65 degrees), which is easy to check
the supplied solution gives explicit intermediate equations that can be rule-checked
